LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA FIRST L.P. AND CALIFORNIA FIRST LLC'S MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE NEW YORK AND SOUTH CAROLINA CASES IS GRANTED ON CONDITION THAT DEFENDANTS AGREE IN A WRITING FILED IN THIS COURT NO LATER THAN JUNE 3, 2016 TO BE BOUND IN THIS CASE AND NOT ATTEMPT TO RE-LITIGATE IN THIS CASE ANY FINAL RULINGS MADE IN THE NEW YORK AND SOUTH CAROLINA CASES. IF DEFENDANTS DO NOT AGREE TO THIS CONDITION, THEIR MOTION FOR A STAY IS DENIED. WHILE A STAY OF THIS CASE MAKES SENSE GIVEN THAT THE NEW YORK AND SOUTH CAROLINA CASES INVOLVE ISSUES THAT NECESSARILY NEED TO BE RESOLVED PRIOR TO THE RESOLUTION OF THE ISSUES IN THIS CASE, A STAY DOES NOT SERVE JUDICIAL ECONOMY IF DEFENDANTS WOULD SEEK TO RE-LITIGATE ISSUES RESOLVED IN THOSE OTHER CASES. MR. GROSS IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND PROVIDE IT TO MR. MALINGAGIO FOR REVIEW. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Malingagio of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton, LLP telephone number 213-620-1780 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Gibbs International.
David Gross of DLA Piper, LLP telephone number 415-836-2562 for Defendant California First, LP.

The Court provided clarification of its tentative ruling. The tentative ruling is confirmed. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA FIRST L.P. and CALIFORNIA FIRST LLC's Motion To Stay this Action pending the conclusion of the New York and South Carolina cases is granted on condition that defendants agree in a writing filed in this court no later than June 3, 2016 to be bound in this case and not attempt to re-litigate in this case any final rulings made in the New York and South Carolina cases. If defendants do not agree to this condition, their motion for a stay is denied. While a stay of this case makes sense given that the New York and South Carolina cases involve issues that necessarily need to be resolved prior to the resolution of the issues in this case, a stay does not serve judicial economy if defendants would seek to re-litigate issues resolved in those other cases. Mr. Gross is to prepare a form of order and provide it to Mr. Malingagio for review.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. PAUL S. MALINGAGIO, ESQ. of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, Tel No. 213-620-1780 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. DAVID F. GROSS, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-836-2562) and DEBORAH E. MCCRIMMON, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-836-2526) of DLA Piper LLP appearing for Defendants California First, LP and California First, LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA FIRST, LP AND CALIFORNIA FIRST, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer to the first and third causes of action for breach of contract and accounting are overruled and their demurrer to the second cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained on misjoinder grounds with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff's first and third claims and the alter ego allegations are adequately pled. Plaintiff's interpretation of the relevant contracts must be accepted as correct on a demurrer unless those contracts are not reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation. The contracts are reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation. Because plaintiff's prayer for relief on its declaratory relief claim seeks the issuance of an equitable lien as to monies that the Antarctica Parties likely also claim entitlement, the Antarctica Parties are necessary parties per Code of Civil Procedure 389(a) and possibly indispensable parties per Code of Civil Procedure 389(b). Plaintiff is given leave to either: 1) delete its prayer for an equitable lien, 2) add the Antarctica Parties as defendants to the second claim, or 3) allege sufficient facts, if they are able to do so, why the Antarctica Parties are not indispensable Parties per Code of Civil Procedure 389(b). All of Plaintiff's objections regarding Defendants' requests for judicial notice are sustained. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAUL S. MALINGAGIO OF SHEPPARD MULLIN LLP, TEL, NO. (213)617-4185 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EDWARD KLEIN OF LINER LLP, TEL. NO. (310)500-3500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. DAVID F. GROSS OF DLA PIPER, TEL. NO. 1-415-836-2562 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. RACHEL B. SHERMAN OF THE PATTERSON BELKNAP & TYLER, TEL. NO. (212)336-2147, COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction - The court denies plaintiff's application for preliminary injunction. First, plaintiff's showing of imminent and irreparable harm is tenuous. Plaintiff is seeking money damages and it is unclear when the settlement proceeds will be paid to the defendants. (Boozer dec., par. 11). Second, it is dubious whether plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary of the Facilitation Agreement or its amendment. The contract merely provides that the funds will be placed with Urban Title Co., Inc. It does not provide that the placement is necessarily for plaintiff's benefit. Third, plaintiff has an adequate remedy in the New York or South Carolina forums. Those forums apparently have jurisdiction over the Antarctica entities. The thrust of this action is to prevent the Antarctica entities from dissipating the settlement funds and leaving plaintiff without a remedy or with a hollow judgment. The New York or South Carolina courts can restrain the Antarctica entities from dissipating the funds. Principles of comity weigh in favor of denying this motion. Fourth, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence showing alter ego or enterprise liability and that argument is improperly raised for the first time in the reply brief. Lastly, the balance of equities and harms weighs in defendants' favor. Based on the instant record, defendants have no interest in the funds that are supposed to go to the Antarctica entities or plaintiff and defendants should not be embroiled in this satellite litigation. The judge at the hearing granted Atty. Rachel Sherman's application to appear as cousel pro-hac vice for the Defendants. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
